title: Epic: Optimize test suite performance
description:
## Overview

Refactor the test suite to improve execution speed while maintaining test quality and isolation.

## Current State

- **17 test files** with ~8,265 lines of test code
- **cli.test.ts** alone is ~4,000 lines (50% of test code)
- Heavy operations per test: temp directory creation, SQLite rebuilds, process spawning
- No parallelization configured
- Property tests run 100 iterations by default

## Goals

1. **Faster local development** - Quick feedback loop with `test:fast`
2. **Parallel execution** - Utilize multi-core CPUs
3. **Smarter test organization** - Split large files for better parallelization
4. **Reduced overhead** - In-memory databases, fewer iterations locally
5. **Measurable improvements** - Baseline metrics to verify gains

## Approach

Batched implementation - all optimizations in one branch, verified together.

## Tasks (in recommended order)

1. Document baseline test metrics
2. Enable Vitest parallelization
3. Add test tags and `test:fast` script (skip integration, keep property tests)
4. Reduce fast-check iterations for local development
5. Add in-memory SQLite option for unit tests
6. Split cli.test.ts by command (~5-7 files)
7. Configure test result caching

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `pnpm test:fast` completes significantly faster than `pnpm test`
- [ ] All tests still pass
- [ ] No test isolation regressions
- [ ] Baseline vs final metrics documented

## Out of Scope

- Shared test fixtures (too risky for test isolation)
- Mocking business logic (violates TDD principles)
close_reason: Epic complete: test suite optimized from 122s to 62s (49% faster), test:fast runs in 5.7s

title: External reviewer: Codex CLI headless integration
description:
## Summary

Add OpenAI Codex CLI as an optional external reviewer in the compound-agent review pipeline. When enabled via config, a dedicated agent template calls codex exec in headless mode after /implementation-reviewer approves, providing a cross-model review perspective.

## Motivation

Same rationale as Gemini integration: cross-model review catches different classes of issues. Codex brings OpenAI reasoning strengths (o-series models) to the review, complementing Claude and Gemini perspectives.

## Design

### Config flag

Add to externalReviewers array in config:

  { "externalReviewers": ["codex"] }

Can coexist with "gemini" -- both run sequentially.

### Agent template

New file: .claude/agents/compound/external-reviewer-codex.md

The agent should:
1. Check command -v codex is available (graceful skip if not)
2. Read the current beads issue context (bd show <id>)
3. Build a context-rich prompt: beads issue description + git diff of session changes
4. Call codex in headless exec mode:
   git diff HEAD~N | codex exec "<review prompt>" --quiet
   Or for structured output:
   codex exec --jsonl "Review the recent changes..."
5. Parse the output (stdout for text mode, JSONL events for structured mode)
6. Present findings as actionable items to the user

### Integration point

- Same as Gemini: runs AFTER /implementation-reviewer returns APPROVED
- Advisory, not blocking
- If both gemini and codex are enabled, run sequentially (gemini first, then codex)

### Codex headless specifics

- Use codex exec (alias codex e) for non-interactive mode
- --quiet for minimal noise, machine-readable output
- --jsonl for event stream (types: item.message, turn.completed, etc.)
- --sandbox workspace-write for safe read-only analysis
- --path . to scope to current repo
- Final answer is on stdout, progress on stderr

### Review prompt template

Same structure as Gemini:
- Beads issue title + description (the WHY)
- Git diff (the WHAT) -- can use --path . instead of piping diff
- Instruction to focus on: correctness, security, missed edge cases, code quality
- Instruction to be concise and actionable
- Instruction to output structured findings (numbered list)

### Setup / activation

- npx ca config set externalReviewers codex (or manual edit)
- Requires: npm i -g @openai/codex + codex login --api-key
- npx ca setup --update deploys the new agent template

### Error handling

- Same pattern as Gemini: graceful skip on any failure
- Check command -v codex before attempting
- Timeout handling (codex can be slow with reasoning models)
- Never block the pipeline on external reviewer failure

## Files to modify

- src/setup/templates/agents-phase11.ts -- add external-reviewer-codex.md template
- src/setup/templates/index.ts -- export new template
- Config reading logic (shared with Gemini feature)
- Workflow templates that orchestrate the review phase

## Testing

- Unit test: agent template exists and has correct structure
- Unit test: config flag parsing (shared with Gemini)
- Integration test: graceful skip when codex not available
- Integration test: review prompt includes beads context + diff

## Dependencies

- Shares config infrastructure with Gemini reviewer feature
- Both features should use a common ExternalReviewer abstraction if possible
close_reason: Closed

title: Claude doesn't use CLI - edits JSONL directly
description:
Claude edits .claude/lessons/index.jsonl directly instead of using CLI.

## Root Cause
AGENTS.md doesn't explicitly prohibit direct edits or emphasize CLI usage.

## Implementation

### 1. Update AGENTS.md template
In `src/commands/setup.ts`, update the AGENTS.md template to include:

```markdown
## CRITICAL RULES

**NEVER edit .claude/lessons/index.jsonl directly.**

The JSONL file is the source of truth and requires:
- Proper ID generation
- Schema validation
- SQLite index sync

Always use CLI commands:
- \`npx lna learn "insight" --severity high\` - Add lesson
- \`npx lna update <id> --insight "new"\` - Modify lesson
- \`npx lna delete <id>\` - Remove lesson
- \`npx lna wrong <id>\` - Mark lesson as invalid

Direct edits will cause:
- Missing IDs
- Schema validation failures
- SQLite index desync
- Lost lessons
```

### 2. Add warning comment in JSONL
When creating index.jsonl, add first line:
```
# DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY - Use 'npx lna learn' command
```

### 3. Validate in prime command
The `lna prime` output should repeat this rule prominently.

### Reference
Pattern: beads AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md "DO NOT use bd edit" section
close_reason: Added CRITICAL RULES section to AGENTS.md template with 'NEVER edit index.jsonl' rule and CLI alternatives

title: Simplify embeddings: use resolveModelFile + switch to EmbeddingGemma-300M
description:
## Problem

The current embedding setup in `src/embeddings/download.ts` is heavyweight compared to OpenClaw's approach:

1. **~90 lines of custom download code** for:
   - Progress reporting
   - Temp file handling with atomic rename
   - Stream backpressure management
   - Manual HuggingFace URL construction

2. **Large model** - nomic-embed-text-v1.5 is ~500MB, heavier than needed for memory search

3. **No fallback options** - if local embedding fails, the system is stuck

## Proposed Solution

### 1. Replace custom download with node-llama-cpp's built-in `resolveModelFile()`:

```typescript
// Before (download.ts + nomic.ts = ~190 lines)
export const MODEL_URL = 'https://huggingface.co/nomic-ai/...';
await downloadFile(MODEL_URL, modelPath);
const model = await llama.loadModel({ modelPath });

// After (~10 lines)
import { resolveModelFile } from 'node-llama-cpp';
const MODEL = 'hf:ggml-org/embeddinggemma-300M-GGUF/embeddinggemma-300M-Q8_0.gguf';
const resolved = await resolveModelFile(MODEL);
const model = await llama.loadModel({ modelPath: resolved });
```

### 2. Switch from nomic-embed-text-v1.5 to EmbeddingGemma-300M:

| Model | Size | Dimensions | Use Case |
|-------|------|------------|----------|
| nomic-embed-text-v1.5.Q4_K_M | ~500MB | 768 | Higher quality, heavier |
| **embeddinggemma-300M-Q8_0** | ~300MB | varies | Lighter, good for memory search |

## Benefits

- **Delete ~90 lines** of download infrastructure
- **~40% smaller model** (~300MB vs ~500MB)
- **Built-in caching** handled by node-llama-cpp
- **Simpler API** - just use `hf:` prefix for HuggingFace models
- **Faster cold start** - smaller model loads faster

## Reference

OpenClaw's implementation: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/src/memory/embeddings.ts

## Scope

- Remove `src/embeddings/download.ts` (or reduce to minimal re-export)
- Rename/update `src/embeddings/nomic.ts` to use `resolveModelFile` + new model
- **Update vector dimension constants** if EmbeddingGemma uses different dimensions
- **Rebuild any existing SQLite indexes** (embedding dimensions may change)
- Update tests
- Update CLI `download-model` command (may become optional/deprecated)
- Update documentation referencing nomic model

## Migration Note

Switching models means existing embeddings in SQLite will be incompatible. Users will need to rebuild their lesson index.
close_reason: Implemented: deleted ~460 lines of custom download code, switched to EmbeddingGemma-300M Q4_0 (~150MB vs ~500MB)

title: EPIC 7: Review Phase
description:
# Implement /compound:review workflow

## Goal
Fourth workflow phase: multi-agent review with inter-communication.
Reviewers can talk to each other. Implementation-reviewer has final authority.

## Scope
- review.md command: workflow instructions for Claude
- review/SKILL.md: reviewer roles, communication patterns, quality gates
- Reviewer agent definitions:
  - security.md, architecture.md, performance.md
  - test-coverage.md, simplicity.md
  - (project-specific reviewers based on tech stack)
- Inter-agent communication: reviewers share findings directly
- Implementation-reviewer gate: mandatory approval before completion
- Findings -> beads issues: P1 findings create blocking beads tasks
- Severity classification: P1 (block), P2 (should fix), P3 (nice to have)

## Exit Criteria
- /compound:review invokable as slash command
- Spawns specialized reviewer team (5+ agents)
- Reviewers communicate findings to each other
- Implementation-reviewer provides final verdict
- P1 findings create blocking beads tasks
- Review report synthesized from all findings
- Integration tests verify the workflow

## Context
- Architecture: doc/ARCHITECTURE-V2.md (Phase 4: Review)
- Agent model: Full team (5-10 reviewers) with inter-communication
- Current review process: .claude/CLAUDE.md (verification subagents)
- Builds on existing implementation-reviewer pattern

title: Redesign prime command with Beads-style trust patterns
description:
# Overview

Redesign `lna prime` to use Beads-style trust language patterns that make Claude believe and follow the injected guidelines.

## Current vs New

| Aspect | Current (load-session) | New (prime) |
|--------|------------------------|-------------|
| Focus | Inject lessons | Teach HOW to use system |
| Language | Neutral | Trust patterns (MUST, CRITICAL) |
| Content | Raw lessons | Guidelines + top lessons |
| Format | Plain text | Structured with protocols |

## Trust Language Patterns (from Beads research)

### 1. Explicit Prohibitions
```markdown
- **Default**: Use lesson_search for complex decisions
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT guess when lessons might help
```

### 2. Emergency Framing
```markdown
# 🔴 Mandatory Recall

You MUST use `lesson_search` BEFORE architectural decisions.
```

### 3. Workflow Sequencing
```markdown
- **Workflow**: Search lessons BEFORE planning, capture AFTER corrections
```

### 4. Consequences
```markdown
**NEVER skip lesson search for complex decisions.** Past mistakes will repeat.
```

## Prime Output Template

```markdown
# Learning Agent Active

> Context Recovery: Run \`lna prime\` after compaction

# 🔴 Mandatory Recall

You MUST use \`lesson_search\` BEFORE:
- Architectural decisions or complex planning
- Patterns you've implemented before in this repo
- After user corrections ("actually...", "wrong", "use X instead")

**NEVER skip lesson search for complex decisions.** Past mistakes will repeat.

## Capture Protocol

Use \`lesson_capture\` AFTER:
- User corrects you
- Test fail → fix → pass cycles  
- You discover project-specific knowledge

**Workflow**: Search BEFORE deciding, capture AFTER learning.

## CLI Commands (manual use)
- \`lna search "query"\` - Find relevant lessons
- \`lna learn "insight"\` - Save new lesson
- \`lna list\` - Show recent lessons

---

## High-Severity Lessons

[TOP 3-5 LESSONS INJECTED HERE]

Format per lesson:
### L001: [trigger]
**Insight**: [insight]
**Tags**: [tags]
```

## Implementation

```typescript
// src/commands/prime.ts
export async function getPrimeContext(): Promise<string> {
  const guidelines = getGuidelinesTemplate();
  const topLessons = await getHighSeverityLessons(5);
  const formattedLessons = formatLessonsForInjection(topLessons);
  
  return `${guidelines}\n\n---\n\n## High-Severity Lessons\n\n${formattedLessons}`;
}

export const primeCommand = new Command('prime')
  .description('Output prime context for hooks (guidelines + top lessons)')
  .action(async () => {
    const context = await getPrimeContext();
    console.log(context);
  });
```

## Token Budget

Target: < 2K tokens total
- Guidelines template: ~500 tokens (fixed)
- Top 5 lessons: ~300 tokens each = 1500 tokens max
- Buffer for formatting: ~200 tokens

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Uses Beads-style trust language patterns
- [ ] Includes mandatory recall section with MUST language
- [ ] Includes capture protocol with workflow
- [ ] Injects top 3-5 high-severity lessons
- [ ] Total output < 2K tokens
- [ ] Works as MCP resource (lessons://prime) and CLI command
close_reason: Implemented Beads-style trust language patterns with comprehensive test coverage. All 41 tests pass. Implementation-reviewer APPROVED.

title: CRITICAL: Pre-commit hook broken - MODULE_NOT_FOUND
description:
# Problem

Pre-commit hook fails with MODULE_NOT_FOUND error:
```
Error: Cannot find module '.../node_modules/learning-agent/dist/cli.js'
```

# Root Cause

User installed from **GitHub URL** instead of **npm**:
- GitHub install: Gets source code WITHOUT dist/ (gitignored)
- npm install: Gets built dist/ folder (included in package)

# Solution

The package is already published to npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/learning-agent

**Fix is documentation + setup command:**

1. Update README to clearly show npm install (not GitHub)
2. Update `lna setup` to warn if installed from GitHub
3. Ensure `lna setup --status` detects this misconfiguration

# Implementation

### 1. README Update
```bash
# Correct (from npm)
pnpm add -D learning-agent

# WRONG (from GitHub - will break hooks)
# pnpm add -D github:user/learning-agent
```

### 2. Setup Detection
In `lna setup` or `lna setup --status`:
```typescript
// Check if dist/ exists
const distExists = fs.existsSync(path.join(__dirname, '../dist'));
if (!distExists) {
  console.error('ERROR: learning-agent was installed from GitHub without dist/');
  console.error('Please reinstall from npm: pnpm add -D learning-agent');
  process.exit(1);
}
```

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] README clearly shows npm install command
- [ ] README warns against GitHub install
- [ ] `lna setup` detects and warns about GitHub install
- [ ] Error message tells user exactly how to fix
close_reason: Implemented install-check utility with 50 tests

title: BUG: JSONL edits not synced to SQLite index
description:
Manual JSONL edits are not reflected in SQLite until explicit rebuild. User added lesson via Edit tool but loadSessionLessons queries JSONL directly, yet the SQLite was out of sync.

**Observed behavior:**
1. User manually added high-severity lesson to index.jsonl
2. New Claude session still said 'No high-severity lessons found'
3. SQLite had different data than JSONL

**Root cause investigation needed:**
- loadSessionLessons reads from JSONL directly via readLessons()
- But other operations might use SQLite
- sync mechanism might not trigger on external edits

**Evidence:**
```
User: 'I've added a high-severity lesson to the JSONL'
Claude: 'SessionStart:startup hook success: No high-severity lessons found.'
```

**Note:** This might actually be a different bug - need to verify if loadSessionLessons really reads JSONL or SQLite
notes:
## Tests to Implement

### Unit Tests
- [ ] loadSessionLessons reads directly from JSONL (verify with mock)
- [ ] Manual JSONL append is immediately visible to readLessons
- [ ] No SQLite dependency for session loading

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Write to JSONL externally -> loadSessionLessons sees it
- [ ] Append lesson via echo >> file -> CLI list shows it
- [ ] SQLite out of sync -> JSONL is source of truth

### Edge Cases
- [ ] JSONL has invalid line -> skip with warning, continue
- [ ] JSONL modified while reading -> consistent snapshot
- [ ] Empty JSONL file -> returns empty array
- [ ] JSONL doesn't exist -> returns empty array (not error)
- [ ] Race condition: two processes appending simultaneously

### Diagnosis Needed
- [ ] Verify loadSessionLessons actually reads JSONL not SQLite
- [ ] Check if there's caching that could cause stale reads
- [ ] Test with 'cat' to JSONL vs 'appendLesson()' function
Investigation complete - NOT a bug.

Root cause: User manual JSONL edit had invalid data (likely type:'quick' without severity field), silently skipped by readLessons().

Code verification:
✅ loadSessionLessons() reads JSONL directly (not SQLite)
✅ No caching layer
✅ JSONL is source of truth

Real issue: Silent parse error handling makes manual edits risky.

Invariants documented: doc/invariants/jsonl_source_of_truth_invariants.md

Recommended follow-up issues:
1. Add 'validate' CLI command
2. Enable parse error logging in CLI
3. Add --strict flag for load-session
close_reason: Investigation complete: NOT A BUG. JSONL-as-source-of-truth is working correctly. User error due to silent parse failures when manually editing JSONL. Created follow-up issues for UX improvements.

title: Auto-sync SQLite after every CLI mutation
description:
Every CLI command that mutates lessons should automatically sync to SQLite.

**Current state:**
- appendLesson() writes to JSONL only
- SQLite requires explicit rebuildIndex() or syncIfNeeded()
- Manual JSONL edits never sync

**Proposed behavior:**
After every mutation (learn, capture, update, delete), automatically:
1. Append to JSONL (current behavior)
2. Call syncIfNeeded() to update SQLite index
3. Optionally regenerate embeddings for changed lessons

**Implementation:**
```typescript
// In cli.ts after appendLesson:
await appendLesson(repoRoot, lesson);
await syncIfNeeded(repoRoot);  // NEW: auto-sync
```

**Commands affected:**
- learn
- capture
- update (new)
- delete (new)
- import

**Performance consideration:**
- syncIfNeeded uses content hash to skip if already synced
- Should be fast for single-lesson operations
- Consider batch mode for import (sync once at end)
notes:
## Tests to Implement

### Unit Tests
- [ ] learn command calls syncIfNeeded after appendLesson
- [ ] capture command calls syncIfNeeded after appendLesson
- [ ] update command calls syncIfNeeded after mutation
- [ ] delete command calls syncIfNeeded after tombstone
- [ ] import command calls syncIfNeeded once at end (not per-lesson)

### Integration Tests
- [ ] After `learn`, lesson immediately searchable via searchKeyword
- [ ] After `update`, changed fields visible in SQLite
- [ ] After `delete`, lesson excluded from SQLite results
- [ ] loadSessionLessons sees newly created high-severity lesson

### Performance Tests
- [ ] Single lesson sync < 100ms
- [ ] Bulk import (100 lessons) + single sync < 5s
- [ ] syncIfNeeded skips when already synced (hash match)

### Edge Cases
- [ ] SQLite file missing -> recreate
- [ ] SQLite file corrupted -> rebuild from JSONL
- [ ] JSONL empty -> sync creates empty tables
- [ ] Concurrent CLI invocations -> no race conditions

title: Update AGENTS.md template with explicit plan-time instruction
description:
## Overview

Enhance the AGENTS.md template injected by `init` command to include explicit plan-time lesson check instructions.

## Background

Claude Code doesn't have a `PlanCreated` hook event. The workaround is to instruct Claude via AGENTS.md to manually run `check-plan` when creating implementation plans.

This is the same approach beads uses - inject instructions into AGENTS.md that Claude reads at session start.

## Current Template

Location: `src/cli.ts` lines 285-331 (`AGENTS_MD_TEMPLATE`)

Current plan-time section:
```markdown
### Retrieval Points

- **Session start**: High-severity lessons loaded automatically
- **Plan creation**: Semantic search for relevant lessons
```

This is too vague. Claude may not know HOW to do semantic search.

## Target Template

```markdown
### Automatic Lesson Loading

High-severity lessons are loaded automatically at session start via Claude Code hooks.

### Plan-Time Lesson Check (REQUIRED)

Before implementing any plan, check for relevant lessons:

\`\`\`bash
npx learning-agent check-plan --plan "brief summary of your plan"
\`\`\`

Display results under a \`## Lessons Check\` heading after your plan.

Example:
\`\`\`
## Implementation Plan
1. Add authentication middleware
2. Create JWT validation
3. Add protected routes

## Lessons Check
- API v2 requires X-Request-ID header (relevance: 0.87)
- Always validate token expiry before processing (relevance: 0.72)
\`\`\`
```

## Requirements

1. **Explicit command**: Show exact command to run
2. **Example output**: Show what Claude should display
3. **REQUIRED label**: Make it clear this is mandatory
4. **Remove ambiguity**: No vague "semantic search" language

## Implementation

Update `AGENTS_MD_TEMPLATE` constant in `src/cli.ts`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Template includes explicit `check-plan` command
- [ ] Template shows example output format
- [ ] Template marks plan-time check as REQUIRED
- [ ] Existing init tests still pass
- [ ] New repos get updated template

## Notes

- This is a template change only
- Existing repos won't be updated (idempotency check sees header exists)
- Users can re-run `init` with manual AGENTS.md edit to get new template

title: Enhance load-session output for Claude context
description:
## Overview

Enhance `load-session` command output to be optimal for Claude Code context injection via SessionStart hooks.

## Background

When SessionStart hook runs `npx learning-agent load-session`, the stdout becomes Claude's context. The output must be:
- Context-efficient (500-1000 tokens max)
- Human-readable (Claude reads it as markdown)
- Gracefully degrading (no errors if no lessons)
- Self-explanatory (Claude understands what it is)

## Current State

Current output (human mode):
```
## Session Lessons (High Severity)

1. [L12345678] Use Polars for files >100MB
   - Source: user_correction (2025-01-28)
   - Tags: performance, data

---
2 high-severity lessons loaded.
```

## Target Output

```markdown
## Lessons from Past Sessions

These lessons were captured from previous corrections and should inform your work:

1. **Use Polars for files >100MB** (performance)
   Learned: 2025-01-28 via user correction

2. **API v2 requires X-Request-ID header** (api, auth)
   Learned: 2025-01-27 via test failure

Consider these lessons when planning and implementing tasks.
```

## Requirements

### Output Format Changes

1. **Header**: `## Lessons from Past Sessions` (more descriptive)
2. **Intro**: Brief explanation of what these are
3. **Lessons**: Bold insight, tags in parens, date + source
4. **Footer**: Actionable reminder
5. **No IDs**: Remove lesson IDs (not useful to Claude)
6. **No [info] prefix**: Clean output for context injection

### Graceful Degradation

When no lessons exist:
```markdown
## Lessons from Past Sessions

No high-severity lessons captured yet. Learn more: npx learning-agent --help
```

**NOT** an error, just informational.

### Silent Failure Mode

When hook runs, errors should not pollute Claude's context:
- Embedding errors: Catch and return empty (load-session doesn't need embeddings anyway)
- File errors: Catch and return empty with warning to stderr
- Parse errors: Skip corrupted lessons (already implemented)

### Token Efficiency

Target: ~100-150 tokens per lesson, ~50 token header/footer
Max lessons: 5 (current default)
Total budget: ~800 tokens max

## Implementation Notes

### Changes to outputSessionLessonsHuman()

Location: `src/cli.ts` lines 253-270

Current:
```typescript
function outputSessionLessonsHuman(lessons: Lesson[], quiet: boolean): void {
  console.log('## Session Lessons (High Severity)\n');
  // ...
}
```

Target:
```typescript
function outputSessionLessonsHuman(lessons: Lesson[], quiet: boolean): void {
  console.log('## Lessons from Past Sessions\n');
  if (!quiet) {
    console.log('These lessons were captured from previous corrections and should inform your work:\n');
  }
  // Format each lesson compactly
  // ...
}
```

### Changes to load-session Command

- Remove `[info]` prefix when no lessons
- Make output cleaner for context injection
- Ensure exit code 0 even with no lessons

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Output format matches target (header, intro, lessons, footer)
- [ ] No lesson IDs in output
- [ ] No `[info]` prefix in output
- [ ] Graceful message when no lessons (not error)
- [ ] Exit code 0 in all cases
- [ ] Token-efficient (~800 tokens max for 5 lessons)
- [ ] Existing tests still pass
- [ ] New tests for enhanced output format

## Testing

1. Test output format with lessons
2. Test output format without lessons
3. Test `--quiet` flag still works
4. Test `--json` output unchanged
5. Verify token count is reasonable
close_reason: Closed

title: Ensure CLI always releases DB/model resources on exit
description:
## Problem
CLI commands can load SQLite and the embedding model but do not call `closeDb()` / `unloadEmbedding()` before exit, contrary to doc/RESOURCE_LIFECYCLE.md.

## Proposed Fix
Add centralized cleanup for CLI process exit:
- Register `process.on('exit'|'SIGINT'|'SIGTERM')` handlers to call `unloadEmbedding()` and `closeDb()` safely.
- Optionally wrap `program.parseAsync()` in a `try/finally` to ensure cleanup on thrown errors.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cleanup runs on normal exit and on SIGINT/SIGTERM.
- [ ] Cleanup safe even if resources never loaded (no throw).
- [ ] No behavioral changes to command outputs.

## Tests
1. (Unit) Stub cleanup functions and assert they are called on process exit handler registration in CLI.
2. (Integration) Run a CLI command that opens DB and ensure process exits cleanly (no open handle warnings).

## Files
- src/cli.ts (cleanup registration)

description:
# Extend lesson schema to unified memory items

## Goal
Evolve from lessons-only storage to a unified memory store that holds
lessons, solutions, patterns, and preferences. All searchable via the
same vector search + ranking.

## Scope
- Extend Zod schema: add solution, pattern, preference types to MemoryItem union
- JSONL backward compatibility: read old lesson format, write new unified format
- SQLite schema update for new fields and types
- Search/retrieval handles all memory types transparently
- MCP tools accept and return all memory types
- Lower capture quality bar (remove actionability gate)
- Compaction handles all types

## Exit Criteria
- [ ] Can store/retrieve all 4 memory item types
- [ ] Old lessons still readable from existing JSONL
- [ ] Vector search returns mixed types ranked by relevance
- [ ] MCP tools work with unified schema
- [ ] Quality filter uses lower bar (novelty + specificity only)
- [ ] All tests pass (existing + new)

title: Compounding subagent: lesson pattern synthesis
description: Core Phase 2 feature. A subagent that runs during or after the compounding phase to synthesize lessons into actionable patterns. TRIGGER: Part of the existing compound workflow (lna compound or similar). Can also be run on-demand. OUTPUT: Updated CCT config file + compacted lesson summaries. This subagent needs access to the lessons store and should use semantic similarity to cluster related mistakes.

title: REFACTOR: Split setup.ts (693 lines → ~150-200 each)
description:
## Problem

`src/commands/setup.ts` is 693 lines, exceeding the 300-line standard by 393 lines.

This violates:
- `.claude/CLAUDE.md`: 'Files < 300 lines'

## Current Structure

The file contains 4 main commands plus many helpers:
1. `init` - Initialize learning-agent in a repo (lines ~50-250)
2. `hooks` - Configure git hooks (lines ~255-400)
3. `setup claude` - Configure Claude Code integration (lines ~405-550)
4. `download-model` - Download embedding model (lines ~555-693)

Plus helper functions:
- `createDirectoryStructure`
- `findFirstTopLevelExitLine`
- `updateClaudeSettings`
- Template generation functions

## Proposed Split

```
src/commands/setup/
├── index.ts           # Re-exports registerSetupCommands (20 lines)
├── init.ts            # init command + directory structure (~180 lines)
├── hooks.ts           # hooks command + git integration (~150 lines)
├── claude.ts          # setup claude command + settings (~180 lines)
├── download-model.ts  # download-model command (~140 lines)
└── templates.ts       # Shared template strings (~100 lines)
```

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Create directory structure
```bash
mkdir -p src/commands/setup
```

### 2. Create `templates.ts`
Extract all template strings:
- AGENTS.md template
- Hook script templates
- Slash command templates

### 3. Create `init.ts`
Extract:
- `init` command and action handler
- `createDirectoryStructure` function
- Directory/file creation logic

### 4. Create `hooks.ts`
Extract:
- `hooks` command and action handler
- Git hook detection/insertion logic
- `findFirstTopLevelExitLine` function

### 5. Create `claude.ts`
Extract:
- `setup claude` command and action handler
- `updateClaudeSettings` function
- Claude settings file manipulation

### 6. Create `download-model.ts`
Extract:
- `download-model` command and action handler
- Model download progress logic

### 7. Create `index.ts`
```typescript
import type { Command } from 'commander';
import { registerInitCommand } from './init.js';
import { registerHooksCommand } from './hooks.js';
import { registerClaudeCommand } from './claude.js';
import { registerDownloadModelCommand } from './download-model.js';

export function registerSetupCommands(program: Command): void {
  registerInitCommand(program);
  registerHooksCommand(program);
  registerClaudeCommand(program);
  registerDownloadModelCommand(program);
}
```

### 8. Update imports
- `src/commands/index.ts` should import from `./setup/index.js`

## Verification

```bash
pnpm build   # Must succeed
pnpm test    # All tests must pass
pnpm lint    # No new violations

# Verify each new file is under 300 lines
wc -l src/commands/setup/*.ts
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `setup.ts` deleted
- [ ] 5-6 new files created in `src/commands/setup/`
- [ ] Each file < 300 lines
- [ ] All CLI commands still work identically
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] No functionality changes
close_reason: Split setup.ts (1022 lines) into 8 files under 300 lines each: claude-helpers.ts (130), claude.ts (142), download-model.ts (55), hooks.ts (190), index.ts (28), init.ts (255), templates.ts (281), types.ts (13). All 984 tests pass.

title: Split cli.test.ts by command
description:
## Purpose

Break the 4,000-line cli.test.ts into smaller files for better parallelization.

## Current State

`cli.test.ts` contains ~55 tests covering all CLI commands in one file. Vitest parallelizes at the file level, so this monolith runs sequentially.

## Target Structure

```
src/cli/
├── add.test.ts         # 'lna add' command tests
├── search.test.ts      # 'lna search' command tests
├── list.test.ts        # 'lna list' command tests
├── show.test.ts        # 'lna show' command tests
├── sync.test.ts        # 'lna sync' command tests
├── compact.test.ts     # 'lna compact' command tests
├── meta.test.ts        # --version, --help, aliases
└── cli-helpers.ts      # Shared test utilities (execCli, tempDir setup)
```

## Shared Test Utilities

Extract common patterns into `cli-helpers.ts`:
```typescript
export function execCli(args: string, cwd: string): string {
  return execSync(`node --import tsx/esm ../dist/cli.js ${args}`, {
    cwd,
    encoding: 'utf-8',
  });
}

export async function setupCliTestDir(): Promise<string> {
  return mkdtemp(join(tmpdir(), 'learning-agent-cli-'));
}
```

## Migration Strategy

1. Create `src/cli/` directory
2. Extract shared utilities first
3. Move tests by command, updating imports
4. Delete original cli.test.ts
5. Verify all tests pass

## Verification

1. Same number of tests as before (55)
2. All tests pass
3. Coverage unchanged
4. Execution faster due to parallelization

title: setup claude should default to project-local, not global
description:
'setup claude' installs to ~/.claude/settings.json (global) by default. This affects ALL projects, not just the one being initialized.

**Observed behavior:**
```
(base) MacBookPro:own_website Nathan$ npx learning-agent setup claude
[ok] Claude Code hooks installed
  Location: ~/.claude/settings.json   <-- GLOBAL!
```

**Expected behavior:**
- Default should be project-local: .claude/settings.json
- Global should require explicit --global flag
- Or at minimum, warn user that this affects all projects

**Rationale:**
- User expects 'init' and 'setup' to affect current project only
- Global hooks load lessons from wrong projects
- Could confuse Claude with irrelevant lessons

**Proposed solution:**
- Change default to project-local
- Add --global flag (currently it's --project for local)
- Swap the semantics: default=local, --global=global
notes:
## Tests to Implement

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Default (no flags) -> installs to .claude/settings.json (project)
- [ ] --global flag -> installs to ~/.claude/settings.json
- [ ] Output shows correct location (project vs global)
- [ ] getClaudeSettingsPath(false) returns project path (BREAKING CHANGE)

### Integration Tests
- [ ] init + setup claude -> both in project directory
- [ ] Global install doesn't affect other projects
- [ ] Project install only affects current project

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Project .claude/ doesn't exist -> create it
- [ ] Running from subdirectory -> finds repo root
- [ ] No git repo -> error or use cwd?
- [ ] Both project and global have hooks -> which takes precedence? (Claude behavior)
- [ ] --uninstall with wrong scope -> helpful error
close_reason: Closed

title: EPIC 6: Work Phase
description:
# Implement /compound:work workflow

## Goal
Third workflow phase: execute the plan using agent teams with adaptive TDD.
Lead delegates beads tasks to worker agents. TDD pairs for non-trivial tasks.

## Scope
- work.md command: workflow instructions for Claude
- work/SKILL.md: TDD workflow, team structure, execution patterns
- Agent definitions: test-writer.md, implementer.md
- Adaptive TDD pair model:
  - Trivial: single agent, no TDD
  - Simple: sequential (test-writer finishes, hands to implementer)
  - Complex: iterative (ping-pong between test-writer and implementer)
- Lead in delegate mode: coordinates, does not code
- Memory injection per agent: each agent gets relevant items for their task
- Beads task lifecycle: claim -> in_progress -> close

## Exit Criteria
- /compound:work invokable as slash command
- Spawns agent team based on plan/beads tasks
- TDD pairs work for simple tasks (sequential handoff)
- TDD pairs work for complex tasks (iterative)
- Lead delegates effectively (no coding by lead)
- Each agent receives relevant memory items
- Beads tasks updated through lifecycle
- Integration tests verify the workflow

## Context
- Architecture: doc/ARCHITECTURE-V2.md (Phase 3: Work)
- Agent model: Team with TDD pairs (adaptive)
- Agent teams docs: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-teams
- Depends on: plan phase output (beads tasks)

title: Add pre-commit hook with lesson reminder for Claude
description:
Implement a pre-commit hook that reminds Claude to consider capturing lessons before committing.

## Background

Based on beads framework analysis, hooks are implemented as:
1. Thin shell scripts in .git/hooks/ that call the main CLI
2. Scripts are installed during 'init' command
3. Hook runs CLI with appropriate arguments, CLI outputs prompt/reminder

## Requirements

1. **Hook installation**: During 'npx learning-agent init', install pre-commit hook shim
2. **Prompt reminder**: Hook outputs a prompt like: 'Before committing, have you captured any valuable lessons from this session? Consider corrections, mistakes, or insights worth remembering.'
3. **Non-blocking**: Hook should NOT block the commit - just output reminder text
4. **Idempotent**: Re-running init should not duplicate hooks
5. **Claude decides**: The prompt is for Claude to decide if lessons are needed

## Implementation Pattern (from beads)

```bash
#!/bin/sh
# .git/hooks/pre-commit (installed by init)
npx learning-agent hooks run pre-commit
```

## CLI Addition

Add 'hooks run <hook-name>' command that outputs the appropriate prompt.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] 'init' command installs pre-commit hook
- [ ] Hook is executable
- [ ] Hook outputs lesson reminder prompt
- [ ] Re-running init does not duplicate hook
- [ ] 'hooks run pre-commit' command works standalone

╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗

║ [ ] Did a test fail → fix → pass cycle reveal an insight?    ║
║ [ ] Did I make an assumption that was wrong?                 ║
║                                                              ║
║ If ANY checkbox applies, capture the lesson NOW:             ║
║   npx lna learn "<insight>" --trigger "<what happened>"    ║
║                                                              ║
║ If NONE apply, you may proceed.                              ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

title: REFACTOR: Split management.ts (759 lines → ~150-200 each)
description:
## Problem

`src/commands/management.ts` is 759 lines, exceeding the 300-line standard by 459 lines.

This violates:
- `.claude/CLAUDE.md`: 'Files < 300 lines'
- `doc/standards/typescript-best-practices.md`: Module structure standards

## Current Structure

The file contains 9 distinct commands:
1. `wrong` - Mark lesson as incorrect (lines ~30-85)
2. `validate` - Restore invalidated lesson (lines ~90-175)
3. `compact` - Compact JSONL storage (lines ~180-260)
4. `stats` - Show lesson statistics (lines ~265-350)
5. `rebuild` - Rebuild SQLite index (lines ~355-400)
6. `export` - Export lessons to file (lines ~405-480)
7. `import` - Import lessons from file (lines ~485-590)
8. `show` - Show single lesson (lines ~595-680)
9. `update` - Update lesson fields (lines ~685-730)
10. `delete` - Delete a lesson (lines ~735-759)

## Proposed Split

```
src/commands/management/
├── index.ts           # Re-exports registerManagementCommands (20 lines)
├── invalidation.ts    # wrong, validate commands (~150 lines)
├── compaction.ts      # compact, rebuild, stats commands (~200 lines)
├── transfer.ts        # export, import commands (~180 lines)
└── crud.ts            # show, update, delete commands (~200 lines)
```

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Create directory structure
```bash
mkdir -p src/commands/management
```

### 2. Create `invalidation.ts`
Extract:
- `wrong` command and action handler
- `validate` command and action handler
- Shared helper: field copying logic (if any)

### 3. Create `compaction.ts`
Extract:
- `compact` command and action handler
- `rebuild` command and action handler
- `stats` command and action handler

### 4. Create `transfer.ts`
Extract:
- `export` command and action handler
- `import` command and action handler

### 5. Create `crud.ts`
Extract:
- `show` command and action handler
- `update` command and action handler
- `delete` command and action handler
- `wasLessonDeleted` helper function

### 6. Create `index.ts`
```typescript
import type { Command } from 'commander';
import { registerInvalidationCommands } from './invalidation.js';
import { registerCompactionCommands } from './compaction.js';
import { registerTransferCommands } from './transfer.js';
import { registerCrudCommands } from './crud.js';

export function registerManagementCommands(program: Command): void {
  registerInvalidationCommands(program);
  registerCompactionCommands(program);
  registerTransferCommands(program);
  registerCrudCommands(program);
}
```

### 7. Update imports
- `src/commands/index.ts` should import from `./management/index.js`

## Verification

```bash
pnpm build   # Must succeed
pnpm test    # All tests must pass
pnpm lint    # No new violations

# Verify each new file is under 300 lines
wc -l src/commands/management/*.ts
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `management.ts` deleted
- [ ] 4 new files created in `src/commands/management/`
- [ ] Each file < 300 lines
- [ ] All CLI commands still work identically
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] No functionality changes

title: BUG: Pre-commit hook appended after exit statements
description:
When pre-commit hook is appended to existing hooks, it's placed AFTER exit statements, meaning it never actually runs.

**Observed behavior:**
User's existing pre-commit hook ends with 'exit 0' or 'exit 1'. The learning-agent block is appended at the very end:
```bash
# ... existing hook code ...
exit 0
fi

# Learning Agent pre-commit hook (appended)
npx learning-agent hooks run pre-commit
```

The learning-agent code is unreachable because the script already exited.

**Expected behavior:**
- Detect existing exit statements and insert before them
- Or wrap the existing hook and ensure our code runs
- Or use a pre-commit framework (husky, etc.)

**Root cause:**
- installPreCommitHook() just appends to file content
- No parsing of existing hook logic

**Impact:** P1 - Pre-commit reminders never shown when user has existing hooks
notes:
## Tests to Implement

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Hook inserted BEFORE 'exit 0' statement
- [ ] Hook inserted BEFORE 'exit 1' statement  
- [ ] Hook inserted BEFORE 'exit $?' statement
- [ ] Multiple exit statements -> inserted before ALL of them
- [ ] No exit statement -> simple append works

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Existing hook with exit still runs our code
- [ ] Existing hook logic preserved and functional
- [ ] Hook output visible during commit

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Exit inside function definition -> don't insert there
- [ ] Exit in heredoc/string -> don't insert there
- [ ] Exit with variable: 'exit $status' 
- [ ] Conditional exit: 'if [ cond ]; then exit 1; fi'
- [ ] Exit at multiple indent levels
- [ ] Hook file has no shebang -> add one?
- [ ] Hook file is binary/not shell -> skip gracefully

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- `src/cli.ts` is significantly smaller (< 200 lines).
- Each command has its own module.
- All tests pass.

title: Evaluate and clamp ranking boost multiplier
description: PRODUCT DECISION: src/search/ranking.ts:65-69. Multiplicative boost: severity(1.5) * recency(1.2) * confirmation(1.3) = max 2.34x. A lesson with moderate similarity (0.4) but all boosts scores 0.936, outranking an excellent match (0.9) with no boosts. Spec defines this formula but extreme cases may produce surprising results. title: Epic: v0.2.4 - Field Testing Fixes
description:
# Epic: v0.2.4 - Hybrid Memory System Redesign

## Vision

Redesign the lesson system to combine the best of **Beads** (trusted hook injection) and **Moltbot** (tool-based memory lookup). The goal is to make Claude **fluent and proactive** with lessons, not just a passive recipient of injected context.

## Problem Statement

Field testing v0.2.3 revealed a core issue: **Claude doesn't trust or effectively use auto-injected lessons** (issue lkij). The current "push" model (inject lessons at session start and plan time) doesn't align with how Claude naturally works.

## Solution: Hybrid Approach

### 1. Keep Trusted Injection (Beads-style)
- SessionStart + PreCompact hooks inject **usage guidelines** + **top high-severity lessons**
- Uses Beads' trust language patterns ("MUST", "CRITICAL", explicit prohibitions)
- Teaches Claude HOW to use the system, not just dumps lessons

### 2. Add Native MCP Tools (Moltbot-style)
- `lesson_search` tool with "Mandatory recall" language in description
- `lesson_capture` tool for saving lessons when mistakes happen
- Claude sees these as first-class tools, not Bash commands to remember

### 3. Remove Plan-Time Hook
- Old `check-plan` hook is removed
- Replaced by tool-based search that Claude uses when needed
- More natural, less noisy

### 4. Add Commit Reminder
- PreCommit hook reminds Claude to capture lessons learned
- Complements reactive capture (when mistakes happen)

## Architecture

```
learning-agent/
├── src/
│   ├── cli.ts              # Commander.js (lna command)
│   ├── mcp.ts              # MCP server (~100 lines, thin wrapper)
│   ├── commands/
│   │   ├── setup.ts        # One-shot setup (MCP + hooks + init + model)
│   │   ├── prime.ts        # Prime injection (guidelines + top lessons)
│   │   └── remind-capture.ts
│   ├── search/             # Shared search logic (used by CLI + MCP)
│   ├── capture/            # Shared capture logic (used by CLI + MCP)
│   └── storage/            # JSONL + SQLite
```

## MCP Server Exposure

| Name | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `lesson_search` | Tool | "Mandatory recall: search lessons BEFORE architectural decisions, after corrections, or when repeating patterns" |
| `lesson_capture` | Tool | "Capture a lesson after: user corrections, test fix cycles, or discovering project-specific knowledge" |
| `lessons://prime` | Resource | Prime context (guidelines + top lessons) for tools that read resources |

## Hook Configuration

| Hook | Command | Purpose |
|------|---------|---------|
| SessionStart | `lna prime` | Inject guidelines + top lessons + CLI reference |
| PreCompact | `lna prime` | Re-inject before compaction (context survival) |
| PreCommit | `lna remind-capture` | Prompt to capture lessons before committing |

## One-Shot Setup (`lna setup`)

Single command configures everything:
1. Register MCP server in `.claude/settings.local.json`
2. Configure all hooks in `.claude/settings.local.json`
3. Initialize `.claude/lessons/index.jsonl`
4. Download embedding model to `~/.cache/learning-agent/`

## Prime Output (What Claude Sees)

```markdown
# Learning Agent Active

> Context Recovery: Run \`lna prime\` after compaction

## Mandatory Recall
You MUST use \`lesson_search\` BEFORE:
- Architectural decisions or complex planning
- Patterns you've implemented before in this repo
- After corrections ("actually...", "wrong", "use X instead")

## Capture Lessons
Use \`lesson_capture\` AFTER:
- User corrects you
- Test fail → fix → pass cycles
- You discover project-specific knowledge

## Commands (for manual use)
- \`lna search "query"\` - Find relevant lessons
- \`lna learn "insight"\` - Save new lesson
- \`lna list\` - Show recent lessons

## High-Severity Lessons
[Top 3-5 lessons auto-loaded here]
```

## Migration

- **Clean break**: New `lna setup` replaces old hooks
- **Lessons preserved**: Existing index.jsonl remains compatible
- **Old hooks removed**: `load-session`, `check-plan` replaced

## Research Sources

- **Beads** (github.com/steveyegge/beads): Hook injection patterns, trust language, CLI documentation format
- **Moltbot/Openclaw** (github.com/openclaw/openclaw): Tool-based memory, mandatory recall language, two-step retrieval

## Success Criteria

1. Claude proactively uses `lesson_search` when making complex decisions
2. Claude captures lessons when corrected without being asked
3. Setup is one command, works out of the box
4. Context cost stays under 2K tokens for prime injection
close_reason: v0.2.4 released: MCP Server, one-shot setup, three-hook system, DRY fixes

title: Implement agent-compliant linter rules for the codebase
description:
## Context

Research from three sources (Anthropic C compiler paper, OpenAI Harness Engineering paper, 
Agentic AI Codebase Guide — all in docs/research/) converges on one key finding:

> "Documentation alone doesn't keep a fully agent-generated codebase coherent." 
> — OpenAI Harness Engineering (Feb 2026)

Our codebase has comprehensive documentation (CLAUDE.md, docs/standards/, docs/verification/) 
that describes ~58 rules/constraints, but **almost nothing is mechanically enforced**. 
The current ESLint config (eslint.config.js) has only 3 basic rules (no-var, prefer-const, 
no-else-return). The custom rules engine (src/rules/) exists but .claude/rules.json is empty.

This is the exact gap OpenAI warns about — rules that exist only as documentation drift and 
become invisible to agents. Agents need **mechanical feedback loops**, not documentation they 
might not read.

## What to implement

### Phase 1: ESLint foundation (existing plugin rules)

Enhance eslint.config.js with rules from existing plugins:

**Security (Inviolable)**:
- `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any` — ban any types
- `@typescript-eslint/explicit-module-boundary-types` — types on all exports
- Custom rule or no-restricted-syntax: no template literals in .prepare()/.run()/.exec()/.all()

**Code quality (Inviolable/Strong Default)**:
- `max-lines-per-function: ["warn", { max: 50, skipBlankLines: true, skipComments: true }]`
- `max-lines: ["warn", { max: 300, skipBlankLines: true, skipComments: true }]`
- `max-depth: ["error", 3]` — enforce early returns over deep nesting
- `@typescript-eslint/no-magic-numbers` — with ignore list [0, 1, -1]

**Test quality**:
- `eslint-plugin-vitest/no-focused-tests` — no .only in tests
- `eslint-plugin-vitest/no-disabled-tests` — no .skip in tests

**Import hygiene**:
- `eslint-plugin-import/no-cycle` — no circular imports
- `eslint-plugin-import/no-commonjs` — enforce ESM

### Phase 2: Custom ESLint rules (agent-compliant)

Create `tools/eslint-rules/` with custom rules following the OpenAI pattern — 
**error messages must include remediation instructions** for agents:

1. **no-sql-interpolation**: Detect template literals passed to SQLite methods.
   Error: "SQL injection risk: Use parameterized query with ? placeholders instead of 
   template literal. See: docs/standards/typescript-best-practices.md#database-sqlite"

2. **no-mock-module-under-test**: In test files, detect vi.mock() where the mocked 
   path matches the test file's corresponding source file.
   Error: "Do not mock the module being tested — test real behavior. Mock only 
   external dependencies. See: docs/standards/anti-patterns.md"

3. **no-utils-helpers-dirs**: Flag files in directories named utils/, helpers/, shared/, 
   common/, or misc/.
   Error: "Utils/helpers directories indicate unclear responsibility. Move this function 
   to the module that owns its concern. See: docs/standards/code-organization.md"

4. **enforce-barrel-exports**: Verify cross-module imports go through index.ts, 
   not internal files.
   Error: "Import from the module's public API (index.ts), not internal files. 
   See: docs/standards/code-organization.md#module-design"

### Phase 3: Structural tests

Add Vitest-based structural tests in `src/__structural__/` that verify project invariants:

- Every src/ subdirectory with .ts files has an index.ts
- index.ts files only contain re-exports (no implementation logic)
- No files/dirs named utils, helpers, shared, common, misc
- .claude/.cache/ is in .gitignore
- package.json has "type": "module"
- No .skip or .only in committed test files (belt-and-suspenders with ESLint)

## Design principles (from the research)

1. **Error messages are agent instructions**: Every lint error should tell the agent 
   exactly what to do and where to find more context. The error IS the remediation.
2. **Mechanical > documentary**: Rules in code beat rules in docs. Promote docs → code 
   whenever possible.
3. **Progressive enforcement**: Start with warnings, escalate to errors once the 
   codebase is clean.
4. **Context window friendly**: Lint output should be concise and grep-friendly 
   (Anthropic paper insight).

## Dependencies

- pnpm add -D eslint-plugin-vitest eslint-plugin-import (Phase 1)
- No external deps for custom rules (Phase 2)
- No external deps for structural tests (Phase 3)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] eslint.config.js enforces all Phase 1 rules
- [ ] pnpm lint passes with zero violations (fix existing code if needed)
- [ ] Custom rules in tools/eslint-rules/ with agent-targeted error messages
- [ ] Structural tests pass in pnpm test
- [ ] Existing test suite still passes (no regressions)
close_reason: Closed

title: Harden custom ESLint rules: barrel-exports bypass, path portability, mock detection
description:
## Context
Implementation review found 3 bugs in custom ESLint rules (findings 3, 4, 5).

## Finding 3 (High): enforce-barrel-exports bypass via extensionless imports
**File**: tools/eslint-rules/rules/enforce-barrel-exports.js:42
**Bug**: `isBarrelImport()` treats any import whose last segment has no dot as a barrel import. Deep extensionless imports like `../memory/storage/sqlite/search` slip through.
**Fix**: Count path segments after the target module root. Only allow single-segment extensionless imports (e.g. `../memory`). Multi-segment extensionless paths (e.g. `../memory/storage/sqlite/search`) must be flagged.

## Finding 4 (Medium): Path portability (Windows)
**Files**: enforce-barrel-exports.js:9 (`/src/` substring), no-utils-helpers-dirs.js:27 (`.split('/')`)
**Bug**: Hardcoded forward-slash path operations fail on Windows.
**Fix**: Use `path.normalize()`, `path.sep`, and `path.relative()` before segment checks in all 3 path-sensitive rules.

## Finding 5 (Medium): no-mock-module-under-test false positives
**File**: tools/eslint-rules/rules/no-mock-module-under-test.js:55-57
**Bug**: Compares only basenames. `vi.mock('../other-dir/search')` in `search.test.ts` falsely matches because basenames collide.
**Fix**: Resolve the mocked relative path against the test file directory and compare normalized absolute candidate paths.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] enforce-barrel-exports correctly flags extensionless deep imports
- [ ] All 3 path-sensitive rules use path.normalize/path.sep
- [ ] no-mock-module-under-test resolves full paths, not just basenames
- [ ] Updated RuleTester tests cover all new edge cases
- [ ] Existing 58 rule tests still pass
close_reason: Closed

title: REFACTOR: Split sqlite.ts (613 lines → ~200-250 each)
description:
## Problem

`src/storage/sqlite.ts` is 613 lines, exceeding the 300-line standard by 313 lines.

This violates:
- `.claude/CLAUDE.md`: 'Files < 300 lines'

## Current Structure

The file contains several distinct concerns:
1. **Schema definition** (~100 lines of SQL)
2. **Connection management** - openDb, closeDb (~80 lines)
3. **Embedding cache** - getCachedEmbedding, setCachedEmbedding, collectCachedEmbeddings (~100 lines)
4. **Index operations** - rebuildIndex, syncIfNeeded (~150 lines)
5. **Search/Query** - searchKeyword, rowToLesson (~100 lines)
6. **Metadata** - getLastSyncMtime, setLastSyncMtime (~50 lines)

## Proposed Split

```
src/storage/sqlite/
├── index.ts           # Re-exports all public functions (30 lines)
├── schema.ts          # SCHEMA_SQL constant + createSchema (~120 lines)
├── connection.ts      # openDb, closeDb, db singleton (~100 lines)
├── cache.ts           # Embedding cache operations (~120 lines)
├── sync.ts            # rebuildIndex, syncIfNeeded (~180 lines)
└── search.ts          # searchKeyword, rowToLesson (~100 lines)
```

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Create directory structure
```bash
mkdir -p src/storage/sqlite
```

### 2. Create `schema.ts`
Extract:
- `SCHEMA_SQL` constant (the entire CREATE TABLE block)
- `createSchema(db)` function
- Any schema-related constants

### 3. Create `connection.ts`
Extract:
- `let db: DatabaseType | null = null` singleton
- `let dbIsInMemory = false` flag
- `openDb(repoRoot, options)` function
- `closeDb()` function
- `DB_PATH` constant

### 4. Create `cache.ts`
Extract:
- `contentHash(text)` function
- `getCachedEmbedding(lessonId)` function
- `setCachedEmbedding(lessonId, embedding, contentHash)` function
- `collectCachedEmbeddings(repoRoot)` function

### 5. Create `sync.ts`
Extract:
- `rebuildIndex(repoRoot)` function
- `syncIfNeeded(repoRoot)` function
- `getLastSyncMtime()` function
- `setLastSyncMtime(mtime)` function

### 6. Create `search.ts`
Extract:
- `searchKeyword(repoRoot, query, limit)` function
- `rowToLesson(row)` function (internal helper)
- `LessonRow` interface

### 7. Create `index.ts`
```typescript
// Re-export public API only
export { DB_PATH, openDb, closeDb } from './connection.js';
export { contentHash, getCachedEmbedding, setCachedEmbedding, collectCachedEmbeddings } from './cache.js';
export { rebuildIndex, syncIfNeeded, getLastSyncMtime, setLastSyncMtime } from './sync.js';
export { searchKeyword } from './search.js';
// Note: rowToLesson is internal, not exported
```

### 8. Update parent index
- `src/storage/index.ts` should import from `./sqlite/index.js`

## Dependency Order

Files should be created in this order due to dependencies:
1. `schema.ts` (no deps)
2. `connection.ts` (imports schema)
3. `cache.ts` (imports connection)
4. `search.ts` (imports connection)
5. `sync.ts` (imports connection, schema, search)
6. `index.ts` (imports all)

## Verification

```bash
pnpm build   # Must succeed
pnpm test    # All tests must pass
pnpm lint    # No new violations

# Verify each new file is under 300 lines
wc -l src/storage/sqlite/*.ts
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `sqlite.ts` deleted
- [ ] 6 new files created in `src/storage/sqlite/`
- [ ] Each file < 300 lines
- [ ] All storage operations still work identically
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] No functionality changes
close_reason: Split sqlite.ts (644 lines) into 8 focused modules under src/storage/sqlite/, all under 300 lines. Backward-compatible shim maintained at sqlite.ts.

title: Pre-commit hook doesn't recall relevant lessons
description:
**Clarification:** This is about whether the pre-commit hook should dynamically query lessons based on staged files, similar to how session-start loads high-severity lessons.

**Current behavior:**
Pre-commit hook outputs a static reminder message (PRE_COMMIT_MESSAGE constant).

**Proposed behavior:**
Hook could:
1. Get list of staged files
2. Query lessons related to those files/topics
3. Inject relevant lessons into Claude's context

**User expectation:**
```
> Now, try to commit something, and tell me if you received some lessons there
⏺ No, I did not receive any lessons or recalls during the commit process.
```

**Considerations:**
- Would slow down every commit (embedding lookup)
- May be noisy if many lessons match
- Pre-commit hook can't easily inject into Claude context (no stdin)
- Better approach might be: Claude manually calls 'check-plan' with commit message

**Decision needed:** Is this feature worth implementing, or is the current static reminder sufficient?

**Alternative:** Improve the static reminder to suggest running 'check-plan' manually
notes:
## Tests to Implement (if feature approved)

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Hook extracts staged file list
- [ ] Hook calls retrieveForPlan with file context
- [ ] Hook formats lessons for output

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Commit with staged Python files -> Python-related lessons shown
- [ ] Commit with no relevant lessons -> graceful message
- [ ] Hook output is captured by Claude

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Many files staged -> reasonable subset used for query
- [ ] No model available -> skip lesson query, show static message
- [ ] Query timeout -> don't block commit

### Performance Tests
- [ ] Hook completes in <500ms for typical commit
- [ ] Large repos don't cause slowdown
close_reason: Not needed - just want static reminder to create lessons

title: FEATURE: Age-based temporal validity (Beads-style)
description:
## Problem
Lessons can become stale over time. The current 30-day recency boost is too crude - it penalizes old lessons but doesn't flag them for review.

## Research Findings (from Beads)
Beads uses a two-tier compaction strategy:
- Tier 1 (30+ days): Basic summarization
- Tier 2 (90+ days): Aggressive archival
- Tracks: compaction_level, compacted_at, original_size
- Dependency-aware: won't compact if dependents still open

## Proposed Solution
1. Add schema fields: `compactionLevel`, `compactedAt`, `lastRetrieved`
2. In `load-session` and `check-plan`, flag lessons >90 days old with warning
3. In `lna stats`, show count of lessons by age bracket
4. Optional: AI summarization for Tier 1 (defer to future version)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Schema includes `compactionLevel` (0=active, 1=flagged, 2=archived)
- [ ] `load-session` shows '⚠️ Old lesson (90+ days)' for flagged lessons
- [ ] `stats` command shows age distribution
- [ ] Tests cover age calculation and flagging logic
close_reason: Closed

title: FIX: init command missing Claude hooks integration (40 failing tests)
description:
## Problem

Tests in `src/cli/init.test.ts` (lines 604-935) expect the `init` command to:
1. Have `--skip-claude` / `--skip-claude-hooks` CLI option
2. Include `claudeHooks: boolean` field in JSON output
3. Integrate with `.claude/settings.json` to install SessionStart hooks

But the actual `init` command (setup.ts lines 719-726) does NOT implement any of these features.

## Impact

- 40 tests failing in full test suite
- 24 tests failing in init.test.ts alone
- Tests pass only when filtered to skip Claude hooks tests

## Root Cause

Tests were written for a feature that was never implemented. The `setup claude` command handles settings.json hooks separately, but `init` was expected to call this functionality as part of the initialization flow.

## Solution Options

### Option A: Implement the feature (recommended)
Make `init` call the same logic as `setup claude` to install hooks automatically:
1. Add `--skip-claude` option to init command
2. Call Claude hooks installation from init
3. Add `claudeHooks` field to JSON output
4. Handle errors gracefully (don't fail init if hooks fail)

### Option B: Remove the tests
If the feature was deprioritized, remove the tests that expect it:
- Delete tests in 'Claude hooks integration (v0.2.1)' describe block
- Update any references to expected behavior

## Related

- `setup claude` command already implements settings.json hook management
- This is NOT related to SQLite graceful degradation work (beads-2f0)
- Tests are pre-existing failures on main branch
close_reason: Fixed: init now integrates Claude hooks, all 984 tests pass

title: Improve Test DX: Execute integration tests against source
description:
Current integration tests in `src/cli.test.ts` rely on the built artifact (`dist/cli.js`).

**Problems:**
- **False Positives/Negatives**: Tests fail or pass based on the *last build*, not the *current code*.
- **Developer Friction**: Requires running `pnpm build` before every test run, slowing down the TDD loop.
- **Confusion**: Debugging is harder as stack traces point to `dist` files.

**Proposal:**
1.  Update test helpers (`runCli`, etc.) to execute `src/cli.ts` directly using `tsx` or `ts-node`.
2.  Ensure `vitest` environment handles the execution correctly.
3.  Remove strict dependency on `dist/` for tests (except perhaps for a final "packaging" test).

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- `pnpm test` passes without a prior `pnpm build`.
- Tests reflect changes in `src/` immediately.

title: check-plan should hard-fail on embedding errors
description:
## Problem
`check-plan` currently catches all errors and prints "No relevant lessons", which violates the spec (embeddings unavailable must hard-fail) and hides real failures.

## Required Behavior
- If embeddings/model are unavailable or resolve fails, `check-plan` must exit non-zero and show a clear error instructing `learning-agent download-model`.
- If there are zero relevant lessons, still exit 0 and print the normal "No relevant lessons" message.
- JSON mode should return a structured error payload and exit non-zero on embedding failure.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Embedding/model failure results in non-zero exit and actionable error message.
- [ ] No blanket catch that masks errors.
- [ ] Empty results still exit 0 with friendly message.
- [ ] JSON output includes `{ error: "..." }` on failure (and exit non-zero).

## Tests
1. When embeddings unavailable, `check-plan` exits non-zero and includes "download-model" in output.
2. When embeddings available, `check-plan` works as before.
3. JSON mode returns error payload on failure.

**Note**: Make the tests conditional on `isModelAvailable()` to avoid CI failures.

## Files
- src/cli.ts (check-plan command)
- src/cli.test.ts (conditional test expectations)
close_reason: check-plan now hard-fails on embedding errors with actionable download-model message

title: Fix git hook message: Distinguish 'Already installed' from 'Not a git repo'
description:
## Overview
The `npx lna init` command shows ambiguous message 'Git hooks: Already installed or not a git repo' which is confusing.

## Current Behavior
```
(base) MacBookPro:test_repo Nathan$ npx lna init
[ok] Learning agent initialized
  ...
  Git hooks: Already installed or not a git repo
```

The user IS in a git repo, but can't tell if the hook was installed or skipped.

## Root Cause
`installPreCommitHook()` returns `false` for BOTH cases:
1. Hook already has our marker (already installed)
2. Not a git repo (no .git directory)

The init command can't distinguish between these cases.

## Fix
Change `installPreCommitHook()` return type from `boolean` to a discriminated union:

```typescript
type HookInstallResult = 
  | { status: 'installed' }
  | { status: 'already_installed' }
  | { status: 'not_git_repo' }
  | { status: 'appended' };  // For existing hooks we appended to
```

Then update init.ts to show appropriate messages:
- 'Git hooks: Installed'
- 'Git hooks: Already installed'
- 'Git hooks: Skipped (not a git repository)'
- 'Git hooks: Appended to existing pre-commit hook'

## Files to Modify
- src/commands/setup/hooks.ts - Change return type and logic
- src/commands/setup/init.ts - Update message display
- src/commands/setup.test.ts - Update tests for new return type

## Testing
- Test: Returns 'installed' for fresh install
- Test: Returns 'already_installed' when marker present
- Test: Returns 'not_git_repo' when no .git directory
- Test: Returns 'appended' when existing hook without marker

title: Require explicit confirmation for capture/detect saves
description:
## Decision (confirmed)
Use explicit confirmation rules:
- **learn** invocation is explicit confirmation → save immediately and set `confirmed: true`.
- **capture**:
  - If TTY and `--yes` not provided → prompt `Save? [y/n]` and save only on `y/yes`.
  - If non-interactive (no TTY) and `--yes` not provided → exit non-zero with guidance to pass `--yes`.
  - `--yes` always saves and sets `confirmed: true`.
- **detect --save** remains, but should be treated as explicit confirmation only when `--yes` is provided (or deprecate/alias to `--yes`).
- `confirmed: false` should not be written by CLI for new saves (only for imports or existing data).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `learn` saves with `confirmed: true` (even without `--yes`).
- [ ] `capture` without `--yes` in non-interactive mode exits non-zero and does not write JSONL.
- [ ] `capture` with `--yes` saves and sets `confirmed: true`.
- [ ] `capture` interactive prompt saves only on `y/yes`, otherwise no write.
- [ ] `detect --save` requires `--yes` (or becomes deprecated alias to `--yes`) and sets `confirmed: true` on save.
- [ ] JSON outputs reflect `saved: true|false` accurately.

## Tests
1. `learn` without `--yes` writes lesson with `confirmed: true`.
2. `capture` without `--yes` in non-interactive CLI returns error and does not write.
3. `capture --yes` writes lesson with `confirmed: true`.
4. `detect --save` without `--yes` errors (or shows deprecation + requires `--yes`).
5. `detect --save --yes` saves with `confirmed: true`.
6. (Optional) Unit test prompt helper: accepts `y/yes`, rejects others.

## Files
- src/cli.ts (capture/learn/detect flows + prompt helper)
- src/cli.test.ts (update expectations + new tests)
close_reason: Capture/detect now require --yes for saves; learn always sets confirmed:true

title: Bump version, tag, and publish to npm
description:
## Overview

Final release steps: bump version, create git tag, publish to npm, create GitHub release.

## Pre-Publish Verification

```bash
# Ensure clean working directory
git status  # Should be clean

# Ensure on main branch
git branch --show-current  # Should be 'main'

# Ensure tests pass
pnpm test:all

# Ensure build works
pnpm build
```

## Version Bump

```bash
# Bump to 0.2.0
pnpm version minor  # or: npm version 0.2.0

# This will:
# - Update package.json
# - Create commit
# - Create git tag v0.2.0
```

## Publish to npm

```bash
# Dry run first
pnpm publish --dry-run

# If dry run looks good, publish
pnpm publish

# Or with OTP if 2FA enabled
pnpm publish --otp=123456
```

## Push to GitHub

```bash
# Push commits and tags
git push origin main
git push origin v0.2.0
```

## GitHub Release

1. Go to: https://github.com/Nathandela/learning_agent/releases/new
2. Select tag: v0.2.0
3. Title: "v0.2.0 - Claude Code Hooks Integration"
4. Description: Copy from CHANGELOG.md
5. Attach: learning-agent-0.2.0.tgz (optional)
6. Publish release

## Post-Publish Verification

```bash
# Verify npm package
npm view learning-agent

# Test install from npm
npm install -g learning-agent
learning-agent --version  # Should show 0.2.0
```

## Checklist

- [ ] Version bumped in package.json
- [ ] Git tag created (v0.2.0)
- [ ] Published to npm
- [ ] Tags pushed to GitHub
- [ ] GitHub release created
- [ ] npm install works globally
- [ ] Announcement posted (if applicable)

## Rollback (if needed)

```bash
# Unpublish (within 72 hours)
npm unpublish learning-agent@0.2.0

# Delete tag
git tag -d v0.2.0
git push origin :refs/tags/v0.2.0
```

title: Claude doesn't trust/follow lessons from hooks
description:
# Problem

Claude doesn't trust or effectively use lessons that are auto-injected via hooks. Field testing showed:
- Claude ignores or forgets injected lessons
- The "push" model doesn't align with how Claude naturally works
- Lessons feel like external noise rather than Claude's own knowledge

# Root Cause Analysis

The current system uses a **push model**: lessons are injected into context at session start and plan time. This creates several issues:

1. **No agency**: Claude is a passive recipient, doesn't "own" the knowledge
2. **Context noise**: Injected lessons may not be relevant to current task
3. **Trust deficit**: Auto-injected content feels less authoritative than tool results
4. **Forgetting**: After compaction or long sessions, injected context fades

# Solution: Hybrid Push-Pull Model

The v0.2.4 redesign addresses this by combining:

## 1. Trusted Injection (Push)
- Keep SessionStart/PreCompact hooks
- But inject **guidelines on HOW to use lessons**, not just raw lessons
- Use Beads-style trust language ("MUST", "CRITICAL")
- Include only top 3-5 high-severity lessons

## 2. Native MCP Tools (Pull)
- `lesson_search` tool Claude can call when needed
- Tool description includes "Mandatory recall" language
- Claude has agency to search, builds ownership
- Results feel more trustworthy (Claude requested them)

## 3. Capture Tool
- `lesson_capture` tool for Claude to take notes
- When Claude captures lessons itself, it owns them
- More likely to remember and trust its own notes

# Why This Should Work

Research from Moltbot shows tool-based memory creates stronger engagement:
- Agent actively decides to search → more trust in results
- "Mandatory recall" language in tool description → consistent behavior
- Two-step retrieval (search then fetch) → keeps context small

Research from Beads shows injection can work when:
- It teaches HOW to use the system, not just dumps data
- Trust language patterns are used consistently
- Clear protocols with explicit consequences

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Claude proactively uses `lesson_search` when making complex decisions
- [ ] Claude captures lessons when corrected without prompting
- [ ] Lessons retrieved via tool feel more trusted than auto-injected
- [ ] Field testing shows improved lesson adherence

title: FEATURE: Manual invalidation CLI (lna wrong)
description:
## Problem
No way to mark lessons as incorrect after retrieval. Bad lessons continue to be retrieved, poisoning agent context.

## Research Findings
- LANDSCAPE.md: 'No Feedback Loop: Missing this memory was wrong mechanism'
- Research synthesis: Most production systems use implicit signals, but explicit invalidation is the simplest lightweight mechanism
- HaluMem shows systems accumulate hallucinations without correction mechanism

## Proposed Solution
1. Add CLI command: `lna wrong <id> --reason 'explanation'`
2. Add schema fields: `invalidatedAt`, `invalidationReason`
3. Invalidated lessons excluded from retrieval by default
4. Add `lna validate <id>` to undo invalidation
5. Add `lna list --invalidated` to show invalidated lessons

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Schema includes `invalidatedAt` (ISO timestamp) and `invalidationReason` (string)
- [ ] `lna wrong <id>` sets invalidatedAt and excludes from retrieval
- [ ] `lna wrong <id> --reason 'text'` stores explanation
- [ ] `lna validate <id>` clears invalidation (re-enables retrieval)
- [ ] `lna list --invalidated` shows invalidated lessons with reasons
- [ ] Invalidated lessons shown with strikethrough or [INVALID] tag in list
- [ ] Tests cover full invalidation/validation cycle

title: Document linting standards for agent-compliant codebases
description:
## Context

Companion to learning_agent-e625 (Implement agent-compliant linter rules). While that issue 
adds mechanical enforcement, this issue creates the documentation standard that explains 
the *philosophy* and *methodology* behind our linting approach.

This is informed by three research papers (docs/research/):
- **Anthropic** (Carlini): Test harness design, context window management
- **OpenAI** (Lopopolo): Custom linters, taste invariants, golden principles, 
  agent-targeted error messages
- **Agentic Codebase Guide**: File/function limits, layered architecture, 
  dependency management

The goal is a reusable standard that goes beyond this project — it should work as 
a reference for any TypeScript codebase built with agentic AI.

## Deliverables

### 1. New standard: `docs/standards/linting-for-agents.md`

A comprehensive document covering:

**A. Philosophy: Why lint differently for agents**
- Agents lack accumulated project knowledge — they depend on mechanical feedback
- Documentation drifts; lint rules don't
- The OpenAI insight: "custom linters with error messages that inject remediation 
  instructions into agent context"
- The Anthropic insight: "context window pollution" — lint output must be concise 
  and actionable, not verbose dumps
- The hierarchy: enforce mechanically > document > hope agents read it

**B. Rule categories and enforcement tiers**

Map our existing tier system (Inviolable / Strong Default / Soft Default / Recommended) 
to enforcement mechanisms:

| Tier | Enforcement | Example |
|------|------------|---------|
| Inviolable | ESLint error (blocks commit) | no-explicit-any, no-sql-interpolation |
| Strong Default | ESLint warn (visible, non-blocking) | max-lines, max-depth |
| Soft Default | Structural test (visible in test run) | no-utils-dirs, barrel exports |
| Recommended | Documentation only | naming conventions, ADR format |

Explain why each tier maps to its enforcement mechanism.

**C. Writing agent-targeted error messages**

A pattern guide for writing lint errors that agents can act on:

Bad: `File too long (312/300)`
Good: `File exceeds 300-line limit (312 lines). Split this module: (1) Extract related 
functions into a new file in same directory, (2) Re-export from index.ts to maintain 
public API, (3) Run pnpm test:fast to verify. See: docs/standards/code-organization.md`

Rules for error messages:
- State the violation (what)
- State the fix (how, step by step)
- Link to documentation (where to learn more)
- Keep under 3 lines (context window budget)

**D. Custom rule development guide**

How to add new custom ESLint rules to tools/eslint-rules/:
- File structure and naming convention
- How to write the AST visitor
- How to write the meta.messages with agent-targeted text
- How to test custom rules
- How to register in eslint.config.js

**E. Structural tests guide**

How to add new structural tests to src/__structural__/:
- What qualifies as a structural test vs a unit test
- Pattern: describe("Project Structure", () => { ... })
- How to verify file existence, import graphs, config correctness
- When to promote a structural test to an ESLint rule

**F. The "golden principles" pattern**

Borrowing from OpenAI: how we encode subjective preferences as mechanical rules:
- Identify a repeated agent mistake
- Define the rule precisely enough to be mechanical
- Write the ESLint rule or structural test
- Write the agent-targeted error message
- Add to eslint.config.js or structural tests
- Document in this standard

This creates a continuous improvement loop: mistake → rule → enforcement → prevention.

### 2. Update existing standards

**docs/standards/README.md**: Add entry for the new document.

**docs/standards/index.md** (if exists): Add to the documentation map.

**docs/standards/code-organization.md**: Add cross-reference to linting-for-agents.md 
for the mechanical enforcement of its rules (file size, function size, module boundaries).

**docs/standards/anti-patterns.md**: Add cross-reference noting which anti-patterns 
now have corresponding lint rules.

### 3. Update docs/research/index.md

Add entries for the three research papers now in docs/research/:
- BuildingACCompilerAnthropic.md
- HarnessEngineeringOpenAi.md  
- AgenticAiCodebaseGuide.md

With a brief synthesis note on the key finding: mechanical enforcement > documentation.

## Design principles

- This document is itself agent-targeted: it should be discoverable, concise, 
  and actionable for Claude Code reading it
- Cross-reference everything (no orphan docs)
- Include concrete examples, not abstract advice
- The standard should be stable enough to reference from CLAUDE.md

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] docs/standards/linting-for-agents.md exists with all sections (A-F)
- [ ] Existing standards updated with cross-references
- [ ] docs/research/index.md updated with all three papers
- [ ] Document is concise (< 400 lines, following our own rules)
- [ ] All cross-references are valid (no broken links)
